next_steps: Perform repetitive nerve stimulation and single-fiber electromyography
Test for anti-acetylcholine receptor antibodies and anti-MuSK antibodies
Obtain a formal swallowing evaluation (videofluoroscopic swallow study)
Consult neurology for a detailed bedside neuromuscular examination
Consider screening for underlying malignancy given the progressive weakness and dysphagia
Continue supportive care with non-invasive ventilation for respiratory insufficiency

differential_diagnoses: Transthyretin cardiac amyloidosis (ATTR) with associated systemic amyloid polyneuropathy
next_steps: Echocardiogram with strain imaging and cardiac MRI to evaluate for infiltrative cardiomyopathy
Serum and urine protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and serum free light chains
Measurement of acid alpha-glucosidase activity in a dried blood spot
Repetitive nerve stimulation and single-fiber EMG
Testing for anti-acetylcholine receptor and anti-MuSK antibodies
Lumbar puncture for protein and cytology to rule out leptomeningeal disease
Genetic testing for TTR gene mutation
